rationale: The app requires a 'Sea World' aesthetic that feels vibrant, playful, and aquatic. We avoid standard 'clinical' or 'tech' dark modes, opting instead for a bright, immersive, and kid-friendly environment using deep navy text, ocean blues, and warm coral/yellow accents.

search_bar.container: backgroundColor: '#FFFFFF', borderRadius: 9999, paddingHorizontal: 20, paddingVertical: 14, flexDirection: 'row', alignItems: 'center'

filter_chip.container: paddingHorizontal: 16, paddingVertical: 8, borderRadius: 9999, borderWidth: 1, marginRight: 8, flexDirection: 'row', alignItems: 'center'

detail_screen.content_sheet: backgroundColor: '#F6FBFC', borderTopLeftRadius: 32, borderTopRightRadius: 32, marginTop: 300, minHeight: '100%', padding: 24

image_urls.description: a black and white fish in a purple sea anemone
an orange and white clown fish in an aquarium

accessibility.contrast: Ensure primary text (#03045E) on backgrounds (#FFFFFF, #F6FBFC) passes AA minimums. Use accent_coral (#EF476F) for poison warnings.
accessibility.testing_attributes: Add testID prop to all touchables (e.g., testID='search-input', testID='filter-chip-omnivore', testID='fish-card-clownfish').
accessibility.touch_targets: Ensure all filter chips and buttons have a minimum height of 44px.

instructions_to_main_agent: This is a React Native Expo application. Use standard StyleSheet.create, NOT Tailwind.
Implement layout using a mobile-first Bento grid concept on the detail screen to show attributes like diet, habitat, and conservation status.
Do not use generic 'Inter' or 'Roboto'. Ensure 'Fredoka' and 'Nunito' are loaded via expo-font.
For the fish list placeholder image, map random indexes of the provided image_urls array, or use the primary one globally until user adds their own.
Apply 'Sea World' vibrance: The background should be Seafoam White, with crisp white cards and colorful yellow/coral/blue tags for filters.
The search bar should have a soft, inviting pill shape. Group filter categories (Color, Diet, Habitat, etc.) clearly.
Use testID attributes strictly in kebab-case for all interactive components.
